text: My debit card was cloned and money was withdrawn from ATM without my knowledge.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC (Cheating) | Section 66C and 66D of IT Act 2000 | Section 318 BNS
what_to_do: Block the card instantly and report the financial fraud to cyber crime authorities.
how_to_do: ["Call your bank's 24/7 helpline to block the card immediately.","Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline at 1930.","File a complaint at cybercrime.gov.in with transaction screenshots.","Submit a written dispute form to your bank manager within 3 days.","If bank refuses liability, approach the Banking Ombudsman under RBI."]
when_to_do: URGENT. Report within 72 hours to ensure zero liability under RBI guidelines.

text: Someone used my credit card for unauthorized international transactions online.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC (Cheating) | Section 66C and 66D of IT Act 2000
what_to_do: Block the card instantly and dispute the transactions with your bank.
how_to_do: ["Call your bank's 24/7 helpline to block the card and raise a dispute.","Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline at 1930.","File at cybercrime.gov.in with all transaction evidence.","Request a chargeback from your bank within 30 days."]
when_to_do: URGENT. Report within 72 hours for zero RBI liability.

text: I became a victim of an online bank fraud phishing attack and lost money.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC | Section 66C and 66D IT Act 2000
what_to_do: Report to cyber crime cell and bank immediately to freeze and recover funds.
how_to_do: ["Call 1930 (National Cyber Crime Helpline) immediately.","File at cybercrime.gov.in with screenshots of the phishing message.","Inform your bank to reverse the fraudulent transaction.","File FIR at local cyber crime police station."]
when_to_do: URGENT — within 72 hours for maximum recovery chances.

text: OTP scam resulted in money being deducted from my savings account without my consent.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC | Section 66C IT Act 2000 | Section 318 BNS
what_to_do: Report the fraud immediately to bank and cyber crime cell.
how_to_do: ["Call 1930 immediately to freeze the fraudulent transaction.","Block your account via net banking or customer care.","File complaint at cybercrime.gov.in.","Visit nearest cyber crime police station with all evidence."]
when_to_do: URGENT — within 72 hours for zero liability.

text: A cheque issued by a customer bounced due to insufficient funds.
ipc_sections: Section 138 of Negotiable Instruments Act 1881
what_to_do: Send a legal demand notice before filing a court case within strict deadlines.
how_to_do: ["Collect the Cheque Return Memo from your bank.","Send a legal demand notice to the issuer within 30 days via Registered Post.","The issuer has 15 days to pay after receiving the notice.","If not paid, file criminal complaint in Magistrate Court within 30 more days."]
when_to_do: Notice MUST be sent within 30 days of receiving return memo. Strict timeline.

text: Business partner gave a post-dated cheque which was dishonored by the bank.
ipc_sections: Section 138 of Negotiable Instruments Act 1881
what_to_do: Send demand notice within 30 days of bounce memo and file Magistrate complaint if unpaid.
how_to_do: ["Collect the Cheque Return Memo.","Send a legal demand notice via Registered Post within 30 days.","Wait 15 days for payment.","If no payment, file criminal complaint in Magistrate Court."]
when_to_do: Strict 30-day deadline from bank return memo.

text: Cheque returned unpaid by bank due to insufficient funds causing financial loss.
ipc_sections: Section 138 Negotiable Instruments Act 1881
what_to_do: Legal demand notice followed by criminal complaint in Magistrate Court.
how_to_do: ["Obtain Cheque Return Memo from bank.","Send demand notice within 30 days via registered post.","File Magistrate complaint if unpaid within 15 days of notice."]
when_to_do: Notice within 30 days of return memo. Case within 30 days after 15-day notice expires.

text: Investment broker cheated me by taking money and never investing it in shares.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC (Cheating) | SEBI Act 1992 | Section 406 IPC (Criminal Breach of Trust)
what_to_do: File complaint with SEBI and police immediately with all transaction records.
how_to_do: ["Gather all receipts, agreements, messages, and bank statements.","File complaint at SEBI online portal (scores.gov.in).","File FIR at local police station for cheating and criminal breach of trust.","Approach Securities Appellate Tribunal if SEBI does not act."]
when_to_do: Immediately. SEBI complaint within 3 years of the fraud.

text: WhatsApp job fraud where I paid money for a job that does not exist.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC (Cheating) | Section 66D IT Act 2000 (Cheating by Personation)
what_to_do: File cyber crime complaint and FIR with all digital evidence preserved.
how_to_do: ["Take screenshots of all WhatsApp conversations and payment receipts.","File complaint at cybercrime.gov.in.","File FIR at local police with all evidence.","Report the phone number/account to the telecom authority."]
when_to_do: Immediately. Digital evidence can be deleted quickly.

text: Ponzi scheme fraud where I lost lakhs of rupees to a fake investment company.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC | Prize Chits and Money Circulation Schemes (Banning) Act 1978 | SEBI Act
what_to_do: Police FIR and SEBI complaint with evidence of the pyramid scheme.
how_to_do: ["Gather all receipts, agreements, and recruitment documents.","File FIR at Economic Offences Wing (EOW) of police.","File complaint with SEBI and/or RBI as applicable.","Join other victims to file a collective complaint for stronger impact."]
when_to_do: Immediately. File before the fraudsters liquidate assets.

text: My tenant is refusing to vacate my house after the lease agreement expired.
ipc_sections: Specific Relief Act 1963 | State Rent Control Act | Section 441 IPC (Criminal Trespass)
what_to_do: Initiate legal eviction proceedings through court. Never use self-help eviction.
how_to_do: ["Send a strict legal eviction notice terminating the tenancy.","Do not cut electricity or water — that is illegal.","If no vacancy, file eviction suit in Rent Controller Court or Civil Court.","Present registered rent agreement as evidence."]
when_to_do: Send legal notice immediately after lease expires.

text: Landlord eviction dispute where tenant has not paid rent for 6 months and won't vacate.
ipc_sections: Specific Relief Act 1963 | State Rent Control Act
what_to_do: File eviction suit and claim arrears of rent through civil court.
how_to_do: ["Issue legal notice for non-payment and eviction demand.","File eviction suit citing non-payment of rent as ground.","Claim rent arrears as part of the suit.","Attach bank statements showing no rent credit."]
when_to_do: File suit immediately. Courts give 30-day notice period to tenant.

text: Landlord is refusing to return my security deposit after I vacated the flat in good condition.
ipc_sections: Indian Contract Act 1872 | Section 403 IPC (Dishonest Misappropriation)
what_to_do: Send written demand notice and pursue civil or consumer court recovery.
how_to_do: ["Send formal emails/messages demanding refund to create paper trail.","Send legal notice via lawyer within 30 days of vacation.","File complaint in Consumer Forum or Civil Court.","Attach move-out inspection photos as evidence of good condition."]
when_to_do: Wait the agreed refund period (15-30 days), then send notice immediately.

text: Neighbor has constructed a boundary wall encroaching on my land without permission.
ipc_sections: Section 441 IPC (Criminal Trespass) | Section 329 BNS | Specific Relief Act 1963
what_to_do: Get official survey done and apply for injunction immediately to stop construction.
how_to_do: ["Apply to Tehsildar for official land measurement and demarcation.","File FIR at local police for criminal trespass.","Approach Civil Court for a Stay Order to stop construction.","File suit for restoration of possession."]
when_to_do: URGENT. Act immediately — once built, demolition is harder to obtain.

text: Builder fraud where possession of flat is delayed indefinitely without any compensation or refund.
ipc_sections: RERA Act 2016 | Consumer Protection Act 2019 | Section 420 IPC
what_to_do: File strong RERA complaint for possession with penalty or full refund with interest.
how_to_do: ["Collect builder-buyer agreement, payment receipts, and brochures.","File complaint with State RERA Authority (online) for possession or refund.","Alternatively, approach NCDRC (National Consumer Commission).","File parallel FIR for cheating against the builder."]
when_to_do: File as soon as builder breaches the promised handover date.

text: Property documents were forged by a relative and property was sold without my consent.
ipc_sections: Section 467/468 IPC (Forgery) | Section 420 IPC (Cheating) | Registration Act 1908
what_to_do: File FIR for forgery and apply for declaration of title immediately.
how_to_do: ["File FIR for forgery and cheating at local police station.","File civil suit for declaration that the sale deed is void.","Apply for an interim injunction to stop any further transfer.","Report to the Sub-Registrar office for cancellation of forged documents."]
when_to_do: Immediately. Do not delay as third-party rights could be created.

text: I paid full amount for a flat but builder is not giving possession and not returning money.
ipc_sections: RERA Act 2016 | Consumer Protection Act 2019 | Section 420 IPC
what_to_do: File RERA complaint and FIR against the builder simultaneously.
how_to_do: ["Compile all payment proofs, agreement and correspondence.","File complaint on State RERA portal demanding possession or refund with 10.85% interest.","File complaint at District Consumer Forum for deficiency of service.","Lodge FIR against builder for cheating and criminal breach of trust."]
when_to_do: File RERA complaint as soon as possession date is breached.

text: My employer terminated me suddenly without giving mandatory notice period or any severance pay.
ipc_sections: Industrial Disputes Act 1947 | State Shops and Establishments Act | Payment of Gratuity Act 1972
what_to_do: Challenge termination legally and claim all pending dues including gratuity.
how_to_do: ["Review offer letter for notice period and termination clause.","Send formal grievance email to HR claiming outstanding salary and severance.","Issue legal notice demanding notice pay, gratuity, and full and final settlement.","File complaint with District Labour Commissioner if company ignores notice.","File case in Industrial Tribunal for unfair termination."]
when_to_do: Send grievance email immediately. Issue legal notice within 15-30 days.

text: Company deducting Provident Fund from salary but not depositing into my EPFO account.
ipc_sections: EPF and MP Act 1952 | Section 405 IPC (Criminal Breach of Trust)
what_to_do: File EPFO grievance and initiate criminal proceedings against employer.
how_to_do: ["Download EPF passbook to confirm missing deposits.","File online grievance on EPFiGMS portal (epfigms.gov.in).","Submit written complaint to Regional PF Commissioner.","File FIR for criminal breach of trust — PF diversion is a criminal offense."]
when_to_do: As soon as you notice missing deposits on the EPFO portal.

text: Senior manager is sexually harassing me in the workplace repeatedly.
ipc_sections: POSH Act 2013 | Section 354A IPC (Sexual Harassment) | Section 74 BNS
what_to_do: File formal complaint with ICC and simultaneously report to police.
how_to_do: ["File written complaint to Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) within 3 months.","Preserve all evidence: emails, WhatsApp, witness names.","If company has no ICC, file with Local Complaints Committee (LCC) at district level.","File Zero FIR at nearest police station under Section 354A."]
when_to_do: File ICC complaint within 3 months of last incident.

text: My salary has not been paid for 3 months and HR is giving excuses.
ipc_sections: Payment of Wages Act 1936 | Minimum Wages Act 1948
what_to_do: Send legal notice and file complaint with Labour Commissioner simultaneously.
how_to_do: ["Collect all salary slips, bank statements and appointment letter.","Send a legal demand notice to the company demanding payment within 15 days.","File complaint with District Labour Commissioner citing Payment of Wages Act.","If company is large, file with Industrial Tribunal directly."]
when_to_do: File complaint within 6 months of wage default.

text: Employer is forcing me to work more than 12 hours per day without overtime payment.
ipc_sections: Factories Act 1948 | Shops and Establishments Act | Payment of Wages Act 1936
what_to_do: Document overtime and file complaint with Factory Inspector or Labour Commissioner.
how_to_do: ["Maintain a log of working hours with time-stamped evidence.","Send written complaint to your employer first.","File complaint with Factory Inspector (for factories) or Labour Commissioner.","Claim overtime at double the wage rate for hours beyond 9 hours/day."]
when_to_do: File complaint within time limit — preferably within 3 months.

text: Company refusing to pay maternity benefits and fired me after I informed about pregnancy.
ipc_sections: Maternity Benefit Act 1961 | Industrial Disputes Act 1947
what_to_do: File complaint immediately — termination during maternity is illegal and punishable.
how_to_do: ["Collect medical certificate, pregnancy record, and termination letter.","File complaint with Inspector under Maternity Benefit Act.","Send legal notice claiming maternity benefits and challenging illegal termination.","File case in Labour Court for reinstatement with back pay."]
when_to_do: File complaint within 1 year of the illegal termination.

text: My landlord is illegally entering my rented apartment without prior notice.
ipc_sections: State Rent Control Act | Section 441 IPC (Criminal Trespass)
what_to_do: Send legal notice and police complaint for illegal entry by landlord.
how_to_do: ["Document each unauthorized entry with timestamps and witnesses.","Send written complaint to landlord demanding compliance with notice requirement.","File police complaint for criminal trespass.","Approach Rent Controller for protection against harassment."]
when_to_do: Send notice immediately after first unauthorized entry.

text: I want to file for mutual consent divorce with my spouse.
ipc_sections: Hindu Marriage Act 1955 Section 13B | Special Marriage Act 1954 Section 28
what_to_do: Draft joint petition with agreed terms on alimony, custody and property.
how_to_do: ["Both parties hire a common lawyer to draft settlement MoU.","Define terms: alimony amount, child custody schedule, property division.","File joint petition in Family Court.","Attend first motion hearing.","Wait 6-month cooling-off period (waivable by Supreme Court if no children).","Attend second motion and get final divorce decree."]
when_to_do: Must have lived separately for at least 1 year before filing.

text: Husband is physically and mentally abusing me and I want divorce.
ipc_sections: Hindu Marriage Act 1955 (Cruelty ground) | Domestic Violence Act 2005 | Section 498A IPC
what_to_do: File for protection immediately and initiate contested divorce proceedings.
how_to_do: ["File complaint with Protection Officer under DV Act for Protection Order.","File FIR under Section 498A for cruelty and dowry harassment.","Engage a family lawyer to file contested divorce petition citing cruelty.","Apply for interim maintenance under Section 125 CrPC for yourself and children."]
when_to_do: File for protection order immediately. Divorce petition can follow.

text: My husband refuses to pay maintenance after separation and I have no income.
ipc_sections: Section 125 CrPC | Hindu Adoptions and Maintenance Act 1956 | Domestic Violence Act 2005
what_to_do: File for interim maintenance in Family Court immediately.
how_to_do: ["File application under Section 125 CrPC in the nearest Magistrate Court.","Provide income proof and evidence of husband's earning capacity.","Apply for interim maintenance pending final hearing.","If DV also exists, apply for maintenance under DV Act as well."]
when_to_do: File for interim maintenance immediately — court can grant it within weeks.

text: My father died without a will and siblings are claiming all the property.
ipc_sections: Hindu Succession Act 1956 | Indian Succession Act 1925
what_to_do: Claim your legal share of ancestral property through civil court proceedings.
how_to_do: ["Gather all property documents: title deed, khata, mutation records.","Identify all legal heirs — Class I heirs have priority under Hindu Succession Act.","Send legal notice to co-heirs asserting your share.","File partition suit in Civil Court if amicable resolution fails.","Apply for succession certificate from court for movable assets."]
when_to_do: File partition suit within 12 years of denial of your share.

text: Daughter-in-law and son are not taking care of my needs and threw me out of house.
ipc_sections: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act 2007
what_to_do: File for maintenance and restoration of residence under Senior Citizens Act.
how_to_do: ["File complaint with District Maintenance Tribunal under the Senior Citizens Act.","Apply for residence protection — children legally cannot throw parents out.","If domestic violence, file DV Act complaint simultaneously.","Courts can order monthly maintenance up to Rs 10,000."]
when_to_do: File complaint immediately with Sub-Divisional Magistrate.

text: I want to adopt a child but don't know the legal process.
ipc_sections: Juvenile Justice Act 2015 | Hindu Adoptions and Maintenance Act 1956 | CARA Guidelines
what_to_do: Register on CARA portal and follow the legally prescribed adoption process.
how_to_do: ["Register on CARA (Central Adoption Resource Authority) portal: cara.wcd.gov.in.","Complete home study by an authorized adoption agency.","Wait for child match — priority given to waiting children.","Appear in court for adoption order.","Complete post-adoption follow-up as required."]
when_to_do: Register on CARA as early as possible — process takes 1-3 years.

text: My in-laws are harassing me for more dowry and threatening me.
ipc_sections: Dowry Prohibition Act 1961 | Section 498A IPC (Cruelty) | Domestic Violence Act 2005
what_to_do: File police complaint and DV Act complaint immediately for protection.
how_to_do: ["Call Women's Helpline 181 or Police 112 immediately if in danger.","File FIR under Section 498A at local police station.","File complaint under Domestic Violence Act with Protection Officer.","Apply for Protection Order and Residence Order in Court.","Document all evidence of harassment: messages, witnesses."]
when_to_do: File complaint immediately. Courts can grant emergency protection orders quickly.

text: I bought a defective mobile phone and company refuses to honor the warranty for repairs.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019
what_to_do: Send legal notice and file complaint in District Consumer Commission.
how_to_do: ["Send legal notice to company/seller giving 15 days to replace or refund.","Register grievance on National Consumer Helpline (NCH) at 1800-11-4000.","File e-Daakhil complaint at District Consumer Commission (edaakhil.nic.in).","Upload invoice, warranty card, and company's refusal communication."]
when_to_do: Consumer complaint must be filed within 2 years of the defect.

text: E-commerce site delivered a fake counterfeit product and rejected my return request.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019 | IT Act 2000
what_to_do: File consumer complaint and cyber complaint for counterfeit product sale.
how_to_do: ["Take photos of the fake product as evidence.","Send legal notice to the seller and platform.","File complaint on National Consumer Helpline.","File e-Daakhil complaint at District Consumer Commission.","Also file at cybercrime.gov.in if online fraud is involved."]
when_to_do: Within 2 years of delivery of the defective product.

text: Hospital charged me excessively and performed unnecessary surgery without proper consent.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019 | Indian Medical Council (Professional Conduct) Regulations
what_to_do: File complaint with Medical Council and Consumer Forum for medical negligence.
how_to_do: ["Gather all medical records, bills, discharge summary, and consent forms.","File complaint with State Medical Council for professional misconduct.","File consumer complaint against hospital for deficiency of service.","For criminal negligence, file FIR under Section 304A IPC (Causing death by negligence)."]
when_to_do: File consumer complaint within 2 years; medical council complaint ASAP.

text: Insurance company rejected my valid medical claim without proper reason.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019 | IRDAI Regulations
what_to_do: File grievance with IRDAI and consumer forum for wrongful claim rejection.
how_to_do: ["File a formal grievance with the insurer's grievance redressal officer.","If unresolved, escalate to IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal (bimabharosa.irdai.gov.in).","File consumer complaint in District Consumer Commission for deficiency of service.","Attach policy documents, claim papers, and rejection letter as evidence."]
when_to_do: File IRDAI complaint within 30 days of claim rejection.

text: Builder sold me a flat without RERA registration and now refuses to execute sale deed.
ipc_sections: RERA Act 2016 | Consumer Protection Act 2019 | Transfer of Property Act 1882
what_to_do: File RERA complaint for mandatory registration and execution of sale deed.
how_to_do: ["Gather all payment receipts and booking agreement.","File complaint with State RERA Authority for unregistered project.","Approach Consumer Forum for deficiency of service.","If fraud suspected, file FIR against builder."]
when_to_do: File RERA complaint immediately — do not wait.

text: Electricity department is charging wrong inflated bills for the past 6 months.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019 | Electricity Act 2003
what_to_do: File grievance with electricity board and approach consumer forum if unresolved.
how_to_do: ["Submit written complaint to the electricity distribution company.","File grievance with State Electricity Regulatory Commission (SERC).","If not resolved, file consumer complaint in District Consumer Commission.","Include all inflated bills as evidence of the overcharging."]
when_to_do: File electricity board complaint first within 30 days. Consumer forum within 2 years.

text: Telecom company is charging me for services I have not subscribed to and ignoring complaints.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019 | TRAI Regulations
what_to_do: File TRAI complaint and consumer forum complaint against the telecom company.
how_to_do: ["File complaint on TRAI CPDR portal (consumercomplaints.trai.gov.in).","Escalate to Telecom Disputes Settlement and Appellate Tribunal (TDSAT) if needed.","File consumer forum complaint for deficiency of service.","Attach bills clearly showing unauthorized charges."]
when_to_do: File TRAI complaint first, then consumer forum within 2 years.

text: Salesperson misrepresented the features of a product and I want a full refund.
ipc_sections: Consumer Protection Act 2019 | Indian Contract Act 1872 (Misrepresentation)
what_to_do: Send legal notice citing misrepresentation and file consumer complaint.
how_to_do: ["Document the misrepresentation: recordings, brochures, written promises.","Send legal notice within 30 days of purchase.","File consumer complaint at District Consumer Commission.","Claim full refund plus compensation for time and harassment."]
when_to_do: Within 2 years of the purchase date.

text: I was hit by a vehicle in a road accident and the driver fled the scene.
ipc_sections: Motor Vehicles Act 1988 | IPC Section 279 (Rash Driving) | Section 337/338 IPC | Solatium Fund Scheme
what_to_do: File FIR, get medical certificate and claim compensation even if driver not caught.
how_to_do: ["Call 112 immediately and note down vehicle number, color, and location.","Get a Medico-Legal Certificate (MLC) from a government hospital.","File FIR at the nearest police station under Section 279 and 337/338 IPC.","Claim compensation from Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT).","If driver not found, claim from Solatium Fund under Motor Vehicles Act."]
when_to_do: File FIR immediately. MACT claim should be filed within 6 months.

text: I was involved in a car accident and the other driver is claiming I was at fault without proof.
ipc_sections: Motor Vehicles Act 1988 | IPC Section 279
what_to_do: Collect evidence and dispute liability — get independent witness statements and CCTV footage.
how_to_do: ["Take photos of the accident scene, vehicle damage, and road conditions.","Collect statements of witnesses present at the scene.","Apply to police for CCTV footage from nearby cameras immediately (footage gets deleted in 7 days).","File counter complaint at police station if you are being wrongly implicated.","Engage a motor accident lawyer to handle insurance dispute and MACT claim."]
when_to_do: File complaint within 24 hours and collect CCTV evidence within 7 days.

text: Insurance company is refusing to pay motor accident claim citing fabricated reasons.
ipc_sections: Motor Vehicles Act 1988 | Insurance Act 1938 | Consumer Protection Act 2019
what_to_do: File grievance with IRDAI and approach MACT for compensation.
how_to_do: ["Gather all: FIR, MLC, medical bills, vehicle RC, and insurance policy.","Send legal notice to insurance company for wrongful repudiation.","File complaint on IRDAI Bima Bharosa portal.","File claim petition in Motor Accident Claims Tribunal (MACT) directly.","Courts can award compensation regardless of insurer's objection."]
when_to_do: File MACT claim within 6 months of accident for faster processing.

text: Drunk driver crashed into my parked vehicle causing major damage and fled.
ipc_sections: Motor Vehicles Act 1988 | IPC Section 279 | Section 304A IPC | Section 185 MV Act (Drunk Driving)
what_to_do: File FIR immediately citing drunk driving and claim insurance compensation.
how_to_do: ["File FIR immediately with vehicle description and any witness accounts.","If driver is identified, police will conduct blood alcohol test (crucial evidence).","Get vehicle damage assessed by an authorized garage in writing.","Claim from your own insurance (own damage) or third-party driver's insurance.","Approach MACT if insurance company disputes claim."]
when_to_do: File FIR within 24 hours. Insurance claim within 7 days of accident.

text: I was wrongfully arrested by police without any evidence or reason.
ipc_sections: Article 22 Constitution of India | Section 57 CrPC | Section 76 BNSS
what_to_do: Exercise constitutional rights immediately and apply for bail in court.
how_to_do: ["Demand to know the reason for arrest — it is your right under Article 22.","Insist on informing a family member or lawyer immediately.","You must be produced before a Magistrate within 24 hours.","Apply for bail immediately — for bailable offenses at police station itself.","File a complaint against illegal detention after release."]
when_to_do: Immediately. Every hour of unlawful detention is a rights violation.

text: I want to file an FIR but the police are refusing to register it.
ipc_sections: Section 154 CrPC | Section 173 BNSS | Section 166A IPC (Public servant disobeying law)
what_to_do: Escalate to SP and Magistrate Court — police must register FIR for cognizable offenses.
how_to_do: ["Get FIR refusal in writing from the police station.","Send written complaint by registered post to Superintendent of Police (SP).","File private complaint directly to Judicial Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC.","File complaint with State Human Rights Commission if rights are violated.","Zero FIR can be filed at any police station regardless of jurisdiction."]
when_to_do: Escalate immediately — SP complaint should be filed same day.

text: Someone posted defamatory content against me on social media spreading lies.
ipc_sections: Section 499/500 IPC (Defamation) | Section 66A IT Act 2000 (Note: SC struck down) | Section 356 BNS
what_to_do: Send legal notice demanding takedown and file defamation complaint.
how_to_do: ["Preserve all evidence: screenshots with URL, date, and time.","Send legal notice to the person demanding removal and public apology.","File criminal defamation complaint in Magistrate Court under Section 499 IPC.","File civil suit for damages if reputation harm is significant.","Request platform (Facebook/Instagram) to remove defamatory content."]
when_to_do: File complaint within limitation period. Preserve evidence immediately before deletion.

text: I am being threatened and extorted by local goons demanding protection money.
ipc_sections: Section 383 IPC (Extortion) | Section 385/386 IPC | Section 308 BNS
what_to_do: File FIR immediately for extortion — it is a serious non-bailable offense.
how_to_do: ["Do NOT pay the extortion money — it encourages more demands.","Preserve all threats: voice recordings, messages, CCTV footage.","File FIR at local police station for extortion under Section 383 IPC.","Request witness protection if you fear retaliation.","If police are unresponsive, file complaint with SP and State Crime Branch."]
when_to_do: File FIR immediately. Do not delay out of fear.

text: My husband is physically beating me and my children at home regularly.
ipc_sections: Domestic Violence Act 2005 | Section 498A IPC | Section 323 IPC (Voluntarily Causing Hurt)
what_to_do: Call helpline, get protection order, and file DV complaint immediately.
how_to_do: ["Call Women Helpline 181 or Police 112 immediately if in danger.","Go to nearest Protection Officer or police station for immediate help.","File complaint under Domestic Violence Act 2005 for Protection Order.","File FIR under Section 498A and 323 IPC.","Apply for emergency shelter if needed through District Protection Officer."]
when_to_do: Immediately. Emergency protection orders can be granted same day.

text: Candidate cheated in my company's recruitment by submitting fake educational certificates.
ipc_sections: Section 420 IPC (Cheating) | Section 467/471 IPC (Forgery) | Section 339 BNS
what_to_do: File police complaint for fraud and forgery and immediately terminate employment.
how_to_do: ["Preserve the fake certificates as evidence.","Terminate the employee immediately citing misrepresentation in offer letter.","File FIR at police station for cheating and use of forged document.","Inform the university named in the fake certificate."]
when_to_do: File FIR promptly after discovering the fraud.

text: My phone was stolen and I want to track it and file a complaint.
ipc_sections: Section 378 IPC (Theft) | Section 303 BNS
what_to_do: File FIR and report IMEI to block the phone and track it.
how_to_do: ["File FIR at nearest police station citing theft and noting the IMEI number.","Block the IMEI by reporting to CEIR portal (ceir.gov.in) — makes phone unusable.","Block your SIM immediately by calling telecom provider.","If found, police can recover the phone with IMEI evidence."]
when_to_do: File FIR within 24 hours — important for insurance claim also.

text: A local gang robbed my shop at gunpoint and took all the cash from the register.
ipc_sections: Section 392 IPC (Robbery) | Section 397 IPC
what_to_do: File an FIR for armed robbery immediately and preserve all CCTV footage.
how_to_do: Call 112 immediately.
Do not touch anything at the crime scene.
File FIR at the local police station.
Hand over CCTV footage to the police.
when_to_do: Immediately

text: Someone I know sexually assaulted and raped me. I am scared to go to the police.
ipc_sections: Section 376 IPC (Rape) | Section 354 IPC
what_to_do: File an FIR under Section 376 immediately and get a medical examination done.
how_to_do: Call Women Helpline 181 or 112.
Go to the nearest hospital for a medical exam.
File a Zero FIR at any police station.
Demand a female police officer for recording your statement.
when_to_do: Immediately to preserve biological evidence.

text: The income tax department sent a notice for tax evasion for my small business.
ipc_sections: Income Tax Act 1961 Section 148
what_to_do: Consult a CA and file a reply to the tax notice within the stipulated time.
how_to_do: Gather all financial records.
Consult a Chartered Accountant or Tax Lawyer.
Draft a formal reply to the IT notice.
File an appeal if a penalty is unjustly levied.
when_to_do: Within the deadline mentioned in the IT notice (usually 15-30 days).
